message:
fix(demo): skip router on retry + tighten ER/orgchart prompts + strip vite auto-regen

Three symptoms from interactive testing last session:

1. ER prompt produced stray '}' in attempt 1 (lost brace-matching
   mid-table); attempts 2+3 ran under router and silently emitted 0
   tokens because the model didn't re-emit setType on retry — the
   router observer discards everything without a setType fire.
2. Orgchart interleaved connect() among addBox() declarations and hit
   'not defined' errors on attempt 1; worked on attempt 2 only because
   the model happened to re-emit setType.
3. bun run rebuild-cache failed when vite's configureServer fired its
   own regen on top of the already-running rebuild — recursion killed
   the outer test's chrome via SingletonLock cleanup.

Fixes:
- main.ts: track lastMountedBranch across attempts. On retry, skip
  router entirely and mount the previous attempt's branch directly.
  Retries now get full think+code under the right specialised KV.
- prompts/preamble.ts: add rule 'declare every node before any
  connect/edge that references it; safe order is nodes first, then
  edges'. Applies to all branches.
- prompts/orgchart.ts: reiterate the declare-before-use rule.
- prompts/er.ts: tighten column-shape spec, add explicit 'exactly
  one closing brace per opening, addTable closes with ])', grow the
  example to 6 tables matching the common e-commerce prompt so the
  model has a closer template.
- vite.config.ts: remove configureServer's auto-regen and all
  supporting infra (checkAndRegen / regenerateCache /
  cleanupPlaywrightChromes / POST /api/write-system-cache). No auto-
  regen anywhere — cache is the committed source of truth; run
  bun run rebuild-cache explicitly on a WebGPU dev box when prompts
  change.

Cache regenerated: 95.9 MB TQKC, 9 branches. bun x tsc clean,
vite build clean in 12s.

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1 @@
-e633aee7f537e8dc
\ No newline at end of file
+fdbce47e3e8c7e45
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -14,44 +14,61 @@ export const ER_PROMPT = `${PREAMBLE}
 
 You are in ER mode (setType("er") has been called).
 Use:
-  addTable(name, columns, opts?)  for every entity. columns is an array of { name, type?, key? }.
+  addTable(name, columns, opts?)  for every entity. columns is a JS array of column objects.
   connect(from, to, "verb", { cardinality: "1:N" })  for every relationship — cardinality opt required.
 
 Do NOT use addBox, addEllipse, addDiamond, addActor, message, addClass, addGroup, or addLane.
 
-ER example:
+Column shape — exactly one of these two forms per column, nothing else:
+  { name: "<col>", key: "PK" | "FK" }                       primary or foreign key
+  { name: "<col>", type: "<sql_type>" }                     regular column
+  { name: "<col>", type: "<sql_type>", key: "PK" | "FK" }   FK with explicit type
+Every column object closes with exactly ONE "}". Every addTable call closes with exactly "]);". Do not add extra "}" inside the columns array.
+
+Valid sql_type values: "int" | "string" | "text" | "decimal" | "timestamp" | "bool" | "uuid".
+
+ER example (6 tables — works one-shot, mirror this structure):
 setType("er");
 const users = addTable("users", [
   { name: "id", key: "PK" },
   { name: "email", type: "string" },
-  { name: "created_at", type: "timestamp" },
+]);
+const categories = addTable("categories", [
+  { name: "id", key: "PK" },
+  { name: "name", type: "string" },
+]);
+const products = addTable("products", [
+  { name: "id", key: "PK" },
+  { name: "name", type: "string" },
+  { name: "category_id", type: "int", key: "FK" },
 ]);
 const orders = addTable("orders", [
   { name: "id", key: "PK" },
   { name: "user_id", type: "int", key: "FK" },
   { name: "total", type: "decimal" },
 ]);
-const items = addTable("order_items", [
+const order_items = addTable("order_items", [
   { name: "id", key: "PK" },
   { name: "order_id", type: "int", key: "FK" },
   { name: "product_id", type: "int", key: "FK" },
   { name: "qty", type: "int" },
 ]);
-const products = addTable("products", [
+const payments = addTable("payments", [
   { name: "id", key: "PK" },
-  { name: "name", type: "string" },
-  { name: "price", type: "decimal" },
+  { name: "order_id", type: "int", key: "FK" },
+  { name: "amount", type: "decimal" },
 ]);
 connect(users, orders, "places", { cardinality: "1:N" });
-connect(orders, items, "contains", { cardinality: "1:N" });
-connect(products, items, "listed in", { cardinality: "1:N" });
+connect(categories, products, "groups", { cardinality: "1:N" });
+connect(orders, order_items, "contains", { cardinality: "1:N" });
+connect(products, order_items, "listed in", { cardinality: "1:N" });
+connect(orders, payments, "settled by", { cardinality: "1:N" });
 
 Rules:
-- Every table MUST have an "id" column with key: "PK" as its first column. Tables without a primary key are invalid.
-- Foreign-key columns are marked with key: "FK" and reference another table's PK.
-- Every connect MUST include { cardinality: "..." } with value "1:1" | "1:N" | "N:1" | "N:M". Unlabelled cardinality is invalid.
-- Connect labels describe the relationship as a verb from source's perspective ("places", "owns", "has", "belongs to").
-- 4+ tables. Every table has at least one relationship (no isolated tables).
-- Column types are short SQL-ish tokens: "string" | "int" | "decimal" | "timestamp" | "bool" | "uuid" | "text" | etc.
+- Every table's FIRST column is the PK: { name: "id", key: "PK" }.
+- FK columns use BOTH type and key: { name: "<parent>_id", type: "int", key: "FK" }.
+- Every connect MUST include { cardinality: "1:1" | "1:N" | "N:1" | "N:M" }. Unlabelled cardinality is invalid.
+- Connect labels are short verbs from source's perspective ("places", "owns", "groups", "contains", "settled by").
+- 4+ tables. Every table participates in at least one relationship.
 
 ${SDK_TYPES}`;

@@ -35,6 +35,7 @@ connect(cto, eng2);
 connect(cfo, fin1);
 
 Rules:
+- Declare EVERY addBox BEFORE any connect call. Never interleave — writing connect(fe_lead, fe_team_1) before const fe_team_1 = addBox(...) throws "fe_team_1 is not defined".
 - One root node (the top of the hierarchy). Every other node must be reachable from the root via parent→child edges.
 - Edges are directional: connect(manager, report). Never connect(report, manager).
 - No cycles. Each report has exactly one manager (no dotted lines / matrix reporting in this mode).
